Article Title: Monsoon Session: Centre calls all-party meet on July 19; opposition readies showdown
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Centre has announced an all-party meet on July 19 ahead of the commencement of Monsoon Session of the Parliament.According to officials, the government is expected to outline its legislative agenda while the opposition parties will raise they intend to focus during the session.The government has a heavy legislative agenda, and several significant bills are expected to be taken up during the session.Meanwhile the opposition is likely to raise the NEET-UG paper leak case and defence minister Rajnath Singh's remarks on casualties in Operation Sindoor. The Congress has moved a breach of privilege notice against the Rajnath Singh."On the recommendation of the Govt of India, Hon'ble President, Smt. Droupadi Murmu ji has approved the summoning of both the Houses of Parliament for the Monsoon Session 2026. The Session will commence on July 20, 2026 and continue till August 13, 2026, for meaningful debate, discussion and decisions on issues of National Importance," Rijiju said in a post on X.The Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) examining the proposed 130th Constitution Amendment Bill is also expected to adopt its report on July 17 before submitting it to Parliament.The Bill has sparked political debate over a provision that mandates the automatic removal of the Prime Minister, Chief Ministers and Union or State ministers from office if they remain in judicial custody for 30 consecutive days in cases involving serious offences.Ahead of the session, several opposition parties have also faced turmoil. After the defeat in the assembly elections, 20 MPs of Trinamool Congress merged with the National Citizens Party of India. Additionally, three party MPs resigned their Rajya Sabha seats and have joined the BJP.There has been further "split" in Shiv Sena (UBT) with six of the party's MPs in the Lok Sabha joining Shiv Sena led by Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde. Seven AAP MPs in the Rajya Sabha had joined BJP earlier.
Article Summary: The government has a heavy legislative agenda, and several significant bills are expected to be taken up during the session. The opposition is likely to raise the NEET-UG paper leak case and defence minister Rajnath Singh's remarks on casualties in Operation Sindoor.

Article Title: Youth killed, friend injured in scooty crash in Maharashtra
Article Text: Navi Mumbai: A 19-year-old youth riding a scooty was killed and his friend riding pillion was severely injured after he crashed into the rear of a truck parked dangerously by the driver on the Mangaon-Shrivardhan stretch of road in Raigad on Saturday around 2.15 pm.The deceased youth was identified as Mohatsim Mubin Dhanse, 19, and the injured pillion rider as Sufiyan Nasir Bandarkar, 19, both residents of Morba village, Mangaon taluka.PSI Sagar Jadhav of Mangaon police station informed that the two youths were riding from Morba towards Mangaon. Upon reaching the river bridge near Rile village about 1 km before Mangaon on the Mangaon-Shrivardhan road, the scooty rider crashed into the rear of a truck parked on the roadside. Due to the impact of the crash, as both were not wearing helmets, they sustained grievous head injuries. While Dhanse sustained injuries to his head and left hand, Bandarkar sustained injuries to the back of his head above the neck. “The trucker had dangerously parked the vehicle by neither switching on the parking light nor keeping the reflector stand behind the truck to alert oncoming vehicles,” Jadhav said.Jadhav said, “Both injured youths were rushed to Mangaon sub-district hospital, where Dhanse was declared brought dead.As Bandarkar was critical, his kin shifted him to Apollo Hospital in Belapur for superspeciality treatment. The accused truck driver was Ishwar Govind Rathod, 30, a resident of Taloja, Navi Mumbai. Rathod claimed that he parked the truck on the roadside and sat inside for eating snacks for his Saturday fasting, for which he purchased potato wafers. ”Jadhav said, “It is possible that the scooty rider Dhanse overtook a heavy vehicle ahead of him from the left side and was caught unawares by the parked truck on the roadside and, unable to control speed, crashed into its rear end.”An FIR was registered against the accused truck driver Rathod under relevant BNS sections for causing the death of a person through a rash or negligent act that does not amount to culpable homicide. Rathod was detained and not arrested but served with a notice under the BNSS provision directing him to co-operate in police probe whenever summoned at the police station and during court trial.
Article Summary: The deceased youth was identified as Mohatsim Mubin Dhanse, 19, and the injured pillion rider as Sufiyan Nasir Bandarkar, 19. The accused truck driver was Ishwar Govind Rathod, 30, a resident of Taloja, Navi Mumbai. Rathod claimed that he parked the truck on the roadside and sat inside for eating snacks for his Saturday fasting.

Article Title: Ran medical camps, made Bollywood contacts: Mumbai man posed as doctor for 28 years, arrested
Article Text: MUMBAI: A 50-year-old man was arrested on Sunday for allegedly posing as a doctor for the last 28 years in Juhu, running medical camps, treating patients, and developing contacts in Bollywood.The impersonation came to notice after Mumbai Crime Branch busted a racket involved in various fraudulent activities and the suspect, Dr Dharmendra Kumar , was called for questioning by DCP (crime) Raj Tilak Roushan. They found that Kumar had allegedly impersonated a doctor, created fake identity documents, opened accounts in various banks and, in collusion with other arrested associates, embezzled some amount through CSR funds over the last three years.Kumar’s arrest was carried out based on information received from an earlier arrested accused, Mohammad Gaus Ibrahim Khatib (52) alias Dr Raj Khatib who was arrested in a cheating case. A Gujarat businessman had lodged a complaint alleging that Khatib, who posed as a DCP in Mumbai Police, a Jaipur businessman and an ‘IPS officer’ cheated him of Rs 16 lakh by promising to recover money owed to him by a builder.Roushan said multiple FIRs were registered against the racket.“In pursuance of the received information, upon finding names of people and companies from Maharashtra and other states ...it has been revealed...that the scope of this crime extends to more than one state. It has also been revealed that an embezzlement of approximately Rs 10 crore has occurred over the last three years,” he said.Roushan said they have seized an identity card, PAN card, visiting cards, and certificates issued by various organisations in the name of Dr Dharmendra Kumar among other items. “Kumar had also visited Khatib’s medical camps which were attended by Bollywood celebrities,” said an officer.Police said the operation was carried out by joint commissioner of police (crime) Anil Kumbhare, additional commissioner of police (crime) Krishna Kant Upadhyay, Roushan and staff.
Article Summary: Mumbai Crime Branch busted a racket involved in various fraudulent activities. The suspect, Dr Dharmendra Kumar, was called for questioning by DCP (crime) Raj Tilak Roushan. They found that Kumar had allegedly impersonated a doctor, created fake identity documents, opened accounts in various banks.

Article Title: Eight duped of Rs 16 lakh on pretext of civic jobs in Maharashtra, FIR on three
Article Text: Thane: Police registered a case against three individuals for allegedly cheating a 49-year-old homemaker and seven other persons of Rs 16 lakh on the promise of securing jobs in the Thane Municipal Corporation (TMC) in Maharashtra , officials said on Sunday.The alleged offence occurred over the last year. The accused allegedly collected Rs 16 lakh from the eight victims after assuring them of jobs in the TMC, but neither arranged the promised jobs nor returned the money, a police officer said. When the victims sought either the promised employment or a refund of their money, the accused allegedly threatened them with dire consequences, he said. Based on the woman’s complaint, the Thane Nagar police registered an FIR on July 10 against the accused under Sections 318(4) (cheating), 316(2) (criminal breach of trust) and 351(2) (criminal intimidation) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, the official said. No arrests have been made so far, and further investigation is underway, the police said.
Article Summary: The alleged offence occurred over the last year. The accused allegedly collected Rs 16 lakh from the eight victims after assuring them of jobs in the TMC. When the victims sought either the promised employment or a refund of their money, the accused allegedly threatened them with dire consequences.

Article Title: Sarpanch, 20 others booked for storming collectorate over BLO duty for ZP teachers
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





A village sarpanch and about 20 others have been booked for allegedly storming the district collector’s office, threatening a tehsildar and disrupting govt work while protesting the appointment of teachers from a zilla parishad school as booth-level officers for the Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar. In a separate case, a private school teacher was booked for allegedly refusing to perform SIR-related booth-level officer duties despite repeated notices.The City Chowk police registered an FIR against Mangesh Sabale, the sarpanch of Gevrai Payga village in Phulambri tehsil, and 20 unidentified people based on a complaint filed by deputy tehsildar Sanjeev Rajur. Sabale was detained by the police.According to the FIR, the group gathered at the district collectorate around 11.30am on Wednesday without permission to oppose the deployment of teachers from the village ZP school for the ongoing SIR.The complainant alleged that the protesters raised slogans, abused the district collector and Phulambri tehsildar, threatened to assault the tehsildar and disrupted official work.The FIR further stated that despite being asked to report to the police station, the protesters remained seated at the collectorate entrance, obstructing public movement. They allegedly threatened to pour petrol on themselves and commit self-immolation if detained.Based on Rajur’s complaint, the police registered a case under sections 132, 296, 126, 189 and 351(2) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and section 135 of the Maharashtra Police Act.In another case linked to the SIR exercise, the Cantonment police booked a private school teacher from the Mukundwadi area for allegedly disobeying election-related orders. A block development officer from Phulambri panchayat samiti alleged that the teacher neither responded to repeated calls from his supervisor nor accepted the appointment order when officials visited his school.The police registered a case under section 223 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and section 32 of the Representation of the People Act for allegedly failing to comply with lawful directions issued for election work.
Article Summary: Mangesh Sabale, the sarpanch of Gevrai Payga village in Phulambri tehsil, and 20 unidentified people have been booked. The group gathered at the district collectorate around 11.30am on Wednesday without permission to oppose the deployment of teachers from the village ZP school. They allegedly threatened to pour petrol on themselves and commit self-immolation if detained.

Article Title: Pressure on Sharad Pawar from within NCP (SP) to pick a political side: Maharashtra minister
Article Text:
Sharad Pawar (File photo)





KOLHAPUR: Senior BJP politician and Maharashtra technical education minister Chandrakant Patil on Saturday said NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar was facing pressure from within his party to take a political decision, either by joining NDA or aligning with a larger opposition party.Addressing reporters in Kolhapur after a meeting with party workers, regarding the Special Intensive Revision of the electoral roll, the BJP minister said various theories were being discussed about the future course of NCP (SP), though he personally had not taken a position on the matter.“In Maharashtra’s politics, there is a tendency to blow up things out of proportion. As a political analyst, I think there is immense pressure on Sharad Pawar to take some decision from within his party, given his age and health conditions. There are numerous possibilities, whether to join any party from NDA or as independent entity in NDA,” Patil said.He said the political timing was also being considered by politicians within the party. “There is two-and-a-half-year term of Lok Sabha left. The assembly election is three years from now. We have our govt in centre and state. Politicians within NCP (SP) are saying that if Sharad Pawar does want to stay in opposition so be it, let us join a bigger entity in the opposition.It is natural to have such pressure within one’s party,” he added.Patil said he preferred to focus on his responsibilities within BJP and the technical education department rather than speculate on the fate of another party.His remarks come amid growing political speculation following reports of recent interactions between senior BJP and NCP (SP) functionaries, including meetings involving former minister Eknath Khadse and BJP functionaries.When asked about the alleged meetings, Patil said political developments are often kept confidential until they are formally announced. “What happened when Sachin Ahir joined Shiv Sena? No one had any idea. When it comes to secrecy, Eknath Shinde does it the best. I wonder how he does it,” Patil said.
Article Summary: BJP politician Chandrakant Patil says NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar is facing pressure from within his party to take a political decision. He said various theories were being discussed about the future course of NCP. Patil said he preferred to focus on his responsibilities within BJP and the technical education department.

Article Title: Panvelkar firing case raised in Maharashtra Council; Sena (UBT) MLA targets govt
Article Text:
MLA raised concerns about a firing incident outside a builder's residence in Maharashtra.





KALYAN: The firing incident outside the office-cum-residence of Ambernath-based builder Vishwanath Panvelkar echoed in the Maharashtra Legislative Council on Thursday, with Shiv Sena (UBT) MLA Ambadas Danve accusing the state government of failing to ensure justice in the case.Raising the issue during the Monsoon Session, Danve alleged that a ruling party MLA from Murbad had given supari to a jailed gangster to intimidate Panvelkar. He claimed that the allegation had surfaced through a letter purportedly written by gangster Ejaz Lakdawala, but no action had been taken despite the disclosure.Danve further alleged that Panvelkar had approached the Prime Minister, the President, the Chief Minister and the police administration seeking justice, but had failed to receive any relief. He claimed that political pressure had influenced the investigation and demanded an impartial probe into the matter.The case dates back to April 21, 2025, when two unidentified motorcycle-borne assailants opened fire outside 'Sitai Sadan', Panvelkar's five-storey office-cum-residence near Hutatma Chowk in Ambernath East. Two rounds were fired, though no one was injured. The Shivajinagar police later arrested the alleged bike rider, Khalid Shahid Sayyed, and the alleged shooter, history-sheeter Jitendra Pawar alias Tiger Bhai.The investigation took a fresh turn in April 2026 after Lakdawala, lodged in jail, allegedly wrote to the Thane police commissioner claiming that associates of BJP MLA Kisan Kathore had approached him with a contract to eliminate Panvelkar over an ongoing land dispute. Panvelkar subsequently accused Kathore of orchestrating the attack.Kathore, however, in past has strongly denied the allegations, describing them as a conspiracy to tarnish his image. He has accused Panvelkar of illegally grabbing land belonging to Adivasi families and has filed a Rs 100-crore defamation suit against the
Article Summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) MLA Ambadas Danve accused the state government of failing to ensure justice in the case. Danve alleged that a ruling party MLA from Murbad had given supari to a jailed gangster to intimidate Panvelkar.

Article Title: Minor pregnant after repeated rape by construction worker in Besa
Article Text: Nagpur: A 17-year-old girl studying in 12th standard was found pregnant after being allegedly sexually exploited multiple times by a 25-year-old construction worker in an under-construction house at Besa Road in Manewada.The accused, Karan Suryavanshi, a native of Rajasthan working at the site and living there, used to wait outside the girl’s college gate daily. He befriended her by expressing his desire to be friends.On Aug 15, 2025, he took her to the site on the pretext of having breakfast, where he raped her. He later dropped her at Sitabuldi MahaMetro station. The two exchanged phone numbers and continued meeting.According to the complaint, Suryavanshi repeatedly took the minor to the same spot and had physical relations with her.After the girl complained of medical complications, her mother took her to a private hospital for a check-up, where doctors confirmed the pregnancy.Based on the victim’s statement, Hudkeshwar police registered a case under Pocso. The accused is not yet arrested.
Article Summary: Karan Suryavanshi, a native of Rajasthan working at the site and living there, used to wait outside the girl’s college gate daily. On Aug 15, 2025, he took her to the site on the pretext of having breakfast, where he raped her. The two exchanged phone numbers and continued meeting.

Article Title: Eknath Khadse rules out switch to BJP, says ‘firmly committed’ to NCP (SP)
Article Text:
Senior NCP (SP) politician Eknath Khadse said he visited Matoshree along with his supporters and party workers to extend a formal invitation to Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray for the celebration of his 75th birthday

Nashik:





Former minister and NCP (SP) MLC Eknath Khadse has dismissed speculation about a possible return to BJP , claiming he will remain with NCP (SP) despite recent meetings that sparked political chatter.Political circles have been abuzz following Khadse’s meeting with BJP national general secretary Vinod Tawde and his subsequent visit to Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray at Matoshree. However, Khadse said neither meeting had any political significance.Strongly rejecting reports of a switch, Khadse said, “I remain firmly committed to my current party.”Explaining his visit to Matoshree, he said, “My supporters and party workers are organising Amrut Mahotsav celebrations next month on the occasion of my 75th birthday, and I visited Matoshree along with my workers to extend a formal invitation to Uddhav Thackeray. It was purely a courtesy meeting without any political agenda.”On his meeting with Tawde, Khadse clarified, “I met Vinod Tawde because he had visited my residence to meet Union minister Raksha Khadse and enquired about my health. No political discussions took place.”Khadse also revealed that the BJP leadership had approached him before the Lok Sabha elections. “I was called by BJP top brass before the Lok Sabha elections to rejoin the party, but after local functionaries threatened to work against the party if I was inducted, the top brass put my entry in the party on hold,” he said.While acknowledging that some of his supporters may join BJP, Khadse stressed that it would not affect his own political course. “I have no plans to switch over to any other party,” he said.Meanwhile, BJP minister Girish Mahajan, a long-time rival of Khadse, reacted to the speculation. “I do not possess the same elite, high-level contacts with the national senior BJP leadership in New Delhi as Khadse does,” he told reporters.Despite Khadse’s denials, the back-to-back meetings have fueled discussion over possible political realignments in Maharashtra ahead of upcoming electoral contests.
Article Summary: Former minister and NCP (SP) MLC Eknath Khadse has dismissed speculation about a possible return to BJP. Political circles have been abuzz following his meeting with BJP national general secretary Vinod Tawde and his subsequent visit to Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray at Matoshree.

Article Title: Case against former legislator for forging signature of senior BJP neta
Article Text:
The police said the accused allegedly prepared four counterfeit letterheads in Gavit’s name and used scanned forged signatures to obtain a corrigendum from the state govt.

Nashik:





The Nandurbar city police have registered a criminal case against a former legislator and three of his associates for allegedly forging the letterhead and signature of senior BJP functionary and former tribal development minister Vijaykumar Gavit to divert Rs 9 crore in govt funds meant for sanctioned development works.The FIR, filed by Ashok Behere, head of Gavit’s city party office, invokes provisions of the BNS related to criminal conspiracy, forgery of documents and use of forged documents as genuine. According to police documents, Gavit, during his tenure as tribal development minister between 2022 and 2024, had secured sanctions exceeding Rs 34 crore from the urban development department for the construction of community halls and temples within Nandurbar Municipal Council limits.Former MP Heena Gavit, the daughter of the former minister, alleged that the accused exploited her father's prolonged illness and hospitalisation to execute the fraud.“Capitalising on his prolonged illness and hospitalisation, the suspects allegedly hatched a criminal conspiracy between August 18, 2025, and April 2, 2026,” she claimed.The police said the accused allegedly prepared four counterfeit letterheads in Gavit’s name and used scanned forged signatures to obtain a corrigendum from the state govt.“This fraudulent manoeuvre successfully diverted Rs 9 crore toward completely different projects for their personal financial and political gains, including Rs 2 crore specifically proposed for a memorial park and hall named after the legislator's late father,” Heena Gavit alleged.She said the alleged fraud came to light after Gavit was hospitalised and subsequent govt orders raised doubts over changes in the sanctioned works.“We approached the department officials after which we came to know about the issue. The order was reversed by the department in favour of the earlier orders. Thereafter, we came to know of several other letters also, and hence we decided to register a FIR on the issue,” Gavit said.According to the complaint, Vijaykumar Gavit had been hospitalised since Jan 2026. The family alleged that while he was undergoing treatment, forged communications were used to alter the allocation of funds originally sanctioned for community infrastructure projects.The police have launched an investigation into the alleged forgery and diversion of funds and are examining the documents submitted to the govt departments concerned.Ends
Article Summary: Former MP Heena Gavit, the daughter of the former minister, alleged that the accused exploited her father's prolonged illness and hospitalisation to execute the fraud. The police have launched an investigation into the alleged forgery and diversion of funds and are examining the documents submitted to the departments concerned.

Article Title: Thirumavalavan blames DMK’s election defeat on its treatment of allies
Article Text:
VCK president Thol Thirumavalavan

Chennai:





VCK president Thol Thirumavalavan on Sunday blamed DMK ’s handling of its allies for the debacle and the subsequent disintegration of the alliance.“If DMK had promised a share in power, allotted more seats to alliance parties and accommodated their seat preferences, it would not have suffered such a setback in the election,” he said at a party event in Tiruvannamalai . “More importantly, the alliance parties would not have left DMK so quickly,” he said.At another event in Dharmapuri, Thirumavalavan took a dig at the Dravidian party for inducting former VCK MLA Panaiyur Babu. “It was done to weaken our party,’’ he said, adding that even if some VCK functionaries had voluntarily approached DMK, they should have been turned away. "That is political ethics,’” he said.Rejecting the criticism that VCK had betrayed DMK, Thirumavalavan said such attacks were an attempt to drive a wedge between DMK and VCK. “Some are trying to position VCK against DMK and even against TVK by misrepresenting my remarks. If ministerial posts were important to us, we would not criticise the govt,” he said.Thirumavalavan also said people should be wary of ‘We The Leaders’ president K Annamalai. “He was trained in RSS camps and cannot speak the politics of Periyar or the Left. If he grows politically, Tamil Nadu could face the fate of Delhi under Arvind Kejriwal or West Bengal under Mamata Banerjee. The BJP helped dislodge the Congress in those states and later emerged as the principal challenger and power contender,” he said.
Article Summary: VCK president Thol Thirumavalavan blamed DMK ’s handling of its allies for the debacle and the subsequent disintegration of the alliance. “If DMK had promised a share in power, allotted more seats to alliance parties and accommodated their seat preferences, it would not have suffered such a setback in the election,’’ he said.

Article Title: Six of family die in car-tanker collision near Tirupur
Article Text:
The accident happened near Uthukuli on Friday afternoon.





COIMBATORE: Six members of a family, including two children, died on the spot and a woman suffered serious injuries after their car collided head-on with a diesel tanker truck near Uthukuli in Tirupur district on Friday afternoon.The deceased have been identified as S Easwaran, 55, of Saminathapuram in Salem, his wife Thirumagal, 50, his son-in-law M Mugilan, 35, who was a college faculty member, Mugilan’s three-year-old son Srinith, his nine-month-old son Kanish, and Easwaran’s relative J Sivagami, 65. Mugilan’s wife Hema, 31, who sustained severe head injuries, was battling for life in the Tirupur Medical College Hospital, police said.According to police, Easwaran and others had gone to Palladam to attend the 30th day ritual of a deceased relative. The accident happened when they were returning to Salem via Uthukuli. Easwaran was driving the car. When the car reached near Kakkapallam close to Uthukuli, a diesel tanker truck, which was coming from the opposite direction, collided head-on with their car. The car was crushed completely, killing six occupants on the spot.After the accident, the tanker truck driver allegedly abandoned the vehicle and fled the scene.On receiving information, the Uthukuli police rushed to the spot, recovered the bodies and sent them to Tirupur Govt Medical College Hospital for postmortem. Police have registered a case and launched a search for the absconding truck driver.
Article Summary: The deceased have been identified as S Easwaran, 55, his wife Thirumagal, 50, his son-in-law M Mugilan, 35, and his two children. Mugilan’s wife Hema, 31, who sustained severe head injuries, was battling for life in the Tirupur Medical College Hospital, police said.

Article Title: Youth held for sexually harassing a woman in Coimbatore
Article Text:
Coimbatore:





A 25-year-old youth was arrested on Thursday, for sexually harassing a woman near Gandhi Nagar in Sundarapuram on Wednesday.The arrested youth has been identified as S Yuvraj, of Sundarapuram. Police said a 29-year-old woman, a resident of Sundarapuram, was walking back home after visiting her relative when the incident took place. “The youth who came on a motorcycle, touched her inappropriately and sped away when she raised an alarm,” said a police officer.However, the woman promptly noted down the motorcycle’s registration number and lodged a complaint with the Sundarapuram police. A case was registered and an investigation initiated.A police team traced the accused after analysing CCTV camera footage and arrested him. He was remanded in judicial custody. Police said he already has a robbery case pending against him.
Article Summary: A 25-year-old youth was arrested on Thursday, for sexually harassing a woman near Gandhi Nagar in Sundarapuram. The arrested youth has been identified as S Yuvraj. Police said he already has a robbery case pending against him.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Luka Doncic dragged into surprising Kylian Mbappe comparison amid France’s World Cup dominance
Article Text: Luka Doncic of the Lakers (Image via Getty)
Article Summary: Luka Doncic is one of the youngest players in NBA history. He was drafted by the L.A. Lakers in the first round of the 2013 NBA draft. He is currently playing for the Slovenian national team in the Euroleague.
